task_completion.description: Load the Mumps.mdb dataset and Survey table into Epi Info 7.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis window showing 'Mumps.mdb' and the 'Survey' table loaded, typically visible in the Data Source section or the output log showing a READ command.

task_completion.description: Perform a frequency analysis on relevant variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Epi Info output window displaying frequency tables (generated via the FREQ command) showing counts and percentages for variables in the dataset.

task_completion.description: Create a 2x2 table of vaccination status versus illness outcome to compute the crude odds ratio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Epi Info output window showing a 2x2 contingency table (generated via the TABLES command) crossing vaccination status and illness, explicitly displaying the crude Odds Ratio (OR).

task_completion.description: Conduct a multivariable logistic regression with illness as the outcome, vaccination status as the primary predictor, and age group/covariates controlled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Epi Info output window showing logistic regression results (generated via the LOGISTIC command) with illness as the dependent variable, displaying Odds Ratios and 95% CIs for vaccination status and covariates.

task_completion.description: Save the complete analysis output to C:\Users\Docker\mumps_analysis.html.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, terminal, or text editor showing the file 'mumps_analysis.html' successfully created in the 'C:\Users\Docker\' directory containing the Epi Info analysis results.

task_completion.description: Export a summary CSV containing variable names, Odds Ratios (OR), and 95% CIs to C:\Users\Docker\mumps_ve_summary.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File explorer, spreadsheet application, or text editor showing 'mumps_ve_summary.csv' in 'C:\Users\Docker\' containing columns for variable names, effect estimates (OR), and 95% CIs.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis to generate the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis GUI (command tree, workspace, or native output window) being interacted with, rather than a Python script or a mocked text file.

integrity.description: Verify the statistical outputs match Epi Info's standard formatting.
integrity.visual_evidence: The HTML output and on-screen results should exhibit the distinct tabular formatting of Epi Info 7 for FREQ, TABLES, and LOGISTIC commands, proving the software actually computed the odds ratios.

integrity.description: Verify the output files were saved to the exact requested paths.
integrity.visual_evidence: The directory paths for the saved HTML and CSV files must explicitly be 'C:\Users\Docker\' and not a default Epi Info project folder or desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis uses specific commands for this workflow: READ (to load data), FREQ (for frequency analysis), TABLES (for 2x2 tables and crude odds ratios), LOGISTIC (for multivariable logistic regression), ROUTEOUT (to save HTML output), and WRITE (to export data). The VLM should look for these commands in the program's command log or their corresponding GUI dialogs. The methodology of using TABLES for 2x2 contingency and LOGISTIC for multivariable regression to find Odds Ratios (OR) and 95% Confidence Intervals (CI) is the standard, verified approach in Epi Info.
